q:
What is prohibited under this bridge or a tunnel?

A. Park on the road and on the verge.
B. Wait and park on the road and on the verge.
C. Wait and park on the road.
e:
LESSON 25 – ‘Waiting and parking’.
You are not allowed to wait or park on the road.

LESSON 25 – ‘Waiting and parking’.
You are not allowed to wait or park on the road.

q:
The cycle lane stops and the cyclists have to ride on the road.

A. You are not allowed to park on the road within 5 meters in front of that place.
B. You are not allowed to park or wait on the road and on the verge within 5 meters in front of that place.
C. You are not allowed to park or wait on the road and on the verge within 5 meters in front of or past that place.

When the cycle lane stops, up to 5 meters in front of and beyond the place where they come on the roadway.

q:
On a speed hump ...

A. ... you may only wait.
B. ... you may wait and park.
C. ... you may not wait and not park
e:
LESSON 25 – ‘Waiting and parking’.
On a speed hump …
- ... you may not wait and not park unless a traffic sign allows it.
- ... is the maximum speed: 30 km / hour.
- ... you may not overtake a vehicle on left (not even a cyclist).

q:
Traffic is moving very slowly and keeps stopping.
If traffic comes to a halt again, are you allowed to stop on an elevated section?

A. Yes.
B. No, you must stop before the elevated section.
e:
LESSON 25
You are not allowed to stop or park on an elevated section, unless road markings or traffic signs indicate otherwise.
This rule is strict.
⚠️ However: in a traffic jam, you may stop briefly to follow the flow of traffic.
This is not considered illegal stopping, but a stop imposed by traffic conditions.

⚠️ However: in a traffic jam, you may stop briefly to follow the flow of traffic.
This is not considered illegal stopping, but a stop imposed by traffic conditions.

q:
May a driver hold his mobile phone to read a message while passengers are getting into the car?

A. Yes.
B. No.
e:
LESSON 25
You may use the device without a holder only if your vehicle is stationary or correctly parked.
The term “stationary” must be interpreted as defined in the Belgian Road Code:
“remaining stationary only for the time strictly necessary to allow passengers to get in or out of the vehicle, or to load or unload the vehicle.”
Briefly stopping at a traffic light, for example, is not considered ‘being stationary’ within the meaning of the Road Code.
clean_q:
May a driver hold his mobile phone to read a message while passengers are getting into the car?
A. Yes.
B. No.
clean_e:
LESSON 25
You may use the device without a holder only if your vehicle is stationary or correctly parked.
The term “stationary” must be interpreted as defined in the Belgian Road Code:
“remaining stationary only for the time strictly necessary to allow passengers to get in or out of the vehicle, or to load or unload the vehicle.”
Briefly stopping at a traffic light, for example, is not considered ‘being stationary’ within the meaning of the Road Code.
